item.text: I’ll trace the resume-candidate and `--resume-last` selection paths, check the repository’s local guidance and existing tests, then patch the narrowest shared/session-aware logic and run the relevant test suite. I’ll leave the working tree uncommitted.

item.text: The mismatch is exactly in the suspected helper: candidate discovery filters by `CODEX_COMPANION_SESSION_ID`, while implicit resume scans every tracked job and always falls back to the app-server thread list. I’m checking the job lifecycle/test harness now so the regression tests exercise both the “foreign completed task” and “foreign running task” cases through the real CLI entry point.

item.text: The two new regression tests failed on the original behavior and now pass after the patch. The implementation now uses the same shared current-session filter for candidate discovery and implicit resume; when a session ID is present, it deliberately does not fall back to the global Codex thread list. I’m moving on to the full suite and build checks.

item.text:
Implemented the session-scoped `--resume-last` fix and left all changes uncommitted.

- Resume selection now filters active and completed tasks by the current Claude session.
- Global thread fallback only occurs when no session ID exists.
- Added regressions for foreign completed and running tasks.
- `npm test`: 66/66 passing.
- `git diff --check` and syntax checks pass.

Modified:

- [codex-companion.mjs](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-maximst-hilaire-Projects-cross-vendor-review-eval/a2d38e60-a217-4ee4-a289-d17e6d6eb038/scratchpad/p1/pos4/authoring/plugins/codex/scripts/codex-companion.mjs:311)
- [job-control.mjs](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-maximst-hilaire-Projects-cross-vendor-review-eval/a2d38e60-a217-4ee4-a289-d17e6d6eb038/scratchpad/p1/pos4/authoring/plugins/codex/scripts/lib/job-control.mjs:15)
- [runtime.test.mjs](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-maximst-hilaire-Projects-cross-vendor-review-eval/a2d38e60-a217-4ee4-a289-d17e6d6eb038/scratchpad/p1/pos4/authoring/tests/runtime.test.mjs:220)

`npm run build` remains blocked by unrelated generated Codex API type drift involving `requestAttestation` and `experimentalRawEvents`.
